betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Space Station Assembler§f is a §6single block§f machine that compiles a player-built station structure into a packed §6Space Station Container§f (advancedrocketry:spacestation) item plus a §6Space Station ID Chip§f (advancedrocketry:spacestationchip). The container can then be launched into orbit by a rocket with a §6Satellite Bay§f to deploy the physical station blocks in space.

§6Production Path (stationbuilder.json)§f:
- Type: §6minecraft:crafting_shaped§f
- Pattern (3×3):
  ```
  u d g
  d s d
  a d a
  ```
- Key: `u`=§6advancedrocketry:misc:0§f (User Interface), `d`=§6dustDilithium§f (forge:ore_dict), `g`=§6gearTitanium§f (forge:ore_dict), `s`=§6advancedrocketry:rocketbuilder§f (Rocket Assembling Machine), `a`=§6advancedrocketry:ic:2§f (Advanced Circuit)
- Output: 1x §6Space Station Assembler§f (advancedrocketry:stationbuilder)

The assembler has the same 3×3 to 16×16 validation bounds as the rocket assembler (TileStationAssembler extends TileRocketAssemblingMachine logic). Build the station frame out of any solid block with a §6Station Docking Port§f (10036) and a §6Station Gravity Controller§f (10037) attached, then right-click the assembler to compile.

The assembler is the prerequisite for the §6Space Station Deployment§f (10035) quest and the §6Station Docking Port§f (10036). It also enables the §6Warp Travel§f (10038) chain by allowing players to construct the §6Warp Core§f room on an orbital station.

§eTip§f: The §6Rocket Assembling Machine§f (`s` key in the recipe) is a hard requirement — the assembler inherits its validation logic. Keep at least one §6Advanced Circuit§f in your inventory when you build, because the assembler will not compile a station without the chip recipe being loaded.
